title: Authenticate Users, Processes, and Devices
description: Authenticate (or verify) the identities of users, processes, or devices as a prerequisite to allowing access to organizational systems.

checks.name: Password Authentication is Required for All Accounts
checks.purpose: Verify no local accounts have blank or empty passwords and all accounts require authentication before access is granted

checks.name: Interactive Logon Requires Authentication
checks.purpose: Confirm the system requires credential entry at interactive logon and automatic logon is disabled

checks.name: Device Authentication is Enforced via Domain Join or Certificate
checks.purpose: Verify the system authenticates to the domain or uses a device certificate to establish identity before network access is granted

checks.name: Password Authentication is Required for All Accounts
checks.purpose: Verify no local or service accounts have blank or empty passwords

checks.name: Interactive Logon Requires Authentication
checks.purpose: Confirm the server requires credential entry at interactive logon and automatic logon is disabled

checks.name: Device Authentication is Enforced via Domain Join
checks.purpose: Verify the server is domain joined and uses Kerberos or certificate-based authentication to establish device identity

checks.name: Service Accounts Require Authentication
checks.purpose: Confirm all service accounts have passwords or use gMSA and none are configured to run without authentication

checks.name: All Accounts Require Password Authentication
checks.purpose: Verify no accounts have empty password fields in /etc/shadow and all interactive accounts require a password

checks.name: PAM Authentication is Enforced for All Login Paths
checks.purpose: Confirm PAM is configured to require authentication for console, SSH, and su access

checks.name: SSH Requires Key or Password Authentication
checks.purpose: Verify sshd_config disables anonymous access and requires either public key or password authentication

checks.name: All Accounts Require Password Authentication
checks.purpose: Verify no accounts have empty password fields in /etc/shadow and all interactive accounts require a password

checks.name: PAM Authentication is Enforced for All Login Paths
checks.purpose: Confirm PAM is configured to require authentication for console, SSH, and su access on Debian

checks.name: SSH Requires Key or Password Authentication
checks.purpose: Verify sshd_config disables anonymous access and requires authentication before granting access

remediation: Ensure all accounts have passwords set. Disable automatic logon. Domain-join systems to enforce Kerberos authentication. On Linux, configure PAM to require authentication for all login paths. Disable anonymous SSH access and ensure all accounts in /etc/shadow have hashed passwords.
